name: Utah vs Idaho Workers Comp Deadlines: Murray vs Boise

mainEntity.name: How do workers comp reporting deadlines differ between Utah and Idaho for injuries in Boise?
mainEntity.text: How do workers comp reporting deadlines differ between Utah and Idaho for injuries in Boise?

acceptedAnswer.text: Utah requires injury reports within 180 days, employer notifies insurer in 7 days, insurer decides in 21 days. Idaho mandates reports within 60 days for medical benefits, 180 days for TTD; employer reports immediately. Cross-state employers must comply with both[1][4].

suggestedAnswer.text: For work injuries spanning Murray UT and Boise ID, Utah's 180-day notice is longer than Idaho's 60 days for medical aid. Failure to meet the stricter deadline risks denial. Consult local rules via state labor commissions; third-party claims may allow lawsuits outside comp. Firm like Just Work Comp Law serves both jurisdictions[1][4].

description: Utah requires injury reports within 180 days, employer notifies insurer in 7 days, insurer decides in 21 days. Idaho mandates reports within 60 days for medical
